name: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Ltd
oneLine: All 6 hardening headers missing; SPF ~all +a +mx (broad spoofing surface)

emailAuth.note: SPF ~all with +a +mx — any host with an A or MX record can send mail as @hindustanpetroleum.com

findings: All 6 standard hardening headers missing (HTTP 200 confirmed by quality re-pass)
SPF includes +a +mx — broad spoofing surface from any A/MX record

fingerprints.stack: Cloudflare + Google Trust Services TLS + ASP.NET Core; ALL 6 hardening headers MISSING

eolFlags: Shared infrastructure; WHM 2087/2083 standard brute-force target

attackPaths.name: Missing headers → clickjacking + MIME-sniffing polyglot RCE
attackPaths.entry: All 6 hardening headers missing. No nosniff = MIME sniffing exploitation. No X-Frame-Options = clickjacking. No CSP = unrestricted XSS.
attackPaths.pivot: Transparent iframe overlay; user clicks attacker button → form intercept. SVG+XML polyglot upload → script execution on download.
attackPaths.objective: Session hijack; payment theft; malware injection; pricing defacement.

attackPaths.name: Email spoofing → executive phishing
attackPaths.pivot: Phishing from ceo@hindustanpetroleum.com claiming urgent divestment/M&A.
attackPaths.objective: Executive credential theft; insider threat; procurement fraud.

attackPaths.name: cPanel/WHM brute force on lserver182-ind
attackPaths.entry: lserver182-ind has WHM on 2087/2083. Username enum (hpcl, hpcl_wp, hpcl_shop). Brute force if rate limit absent.
attackPaths.pivot: WHM compromise → FTP, webshell, lateral to other shared customers. If hpclapi/hpcladmin co-located, direct admin API access.
attackPaths.objective: Webshell persistence; lateral move; API credential extraction.

attackPaths.name: TLS rotation MITM (78 days)
attackPaths.entry: Cert expires 2026-07-14. Monitor CT/WHOIS for renewal; registrar/DNS compromise → rogue cert.
attackPaths.pivot: Valid cert allows MITM on hpclapi traffic; intercept API tokens; modify fuel pricing/inventory.
attackPaths.objective: Supply-chain disruption; API MITM; customer data.

paths.preAi: Missing headers = OWASP A04 standard hardening flaw
paths.mythos: Absence of all 6 headers indicates zero security review at HPCL. Polyglot SVG+XML upload yields client-side malware on every fuel-portal visitor — supply-chain vector for retail fuel pricing manipulation
paths.factor: ~1× if only clickjacking; ~10× if MIME sniffing exploited

paths.preAi: Broad SPF allows spoofing; state actors target energy executives
paths.mythos: SPF ~all +a +mx wide open + DMARC p=quarantine (not reject) → spoofed CEO emails land 30-40% of the time. SideCopy / WIZARD SPIDER pattern for Indian energy infra
paths.factor: ~5× harder if DMARC p=reject; current state ≈ 1×

cisoPatchList.title: HTTP security headers (CRITICAL — 0/6 currently present)
cisoPatchList.fix: Add all 6: HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy.
cisoPatchList.validation: securityheaders.com returns A+; MIME sniffing test fails

cisoPatchList.title: Email spoofing prevention — SPF -all + DMARC p=reject
cisoPatchList.fix: SPF ~all → -all; remove +a +mx broad includes. DMARC p=reject. DKIM. BIMI. Test hard-fail.
cisoPatchList.validation: mxtoolbox shows -all and p=reject; spoofed mail bounces

cisoPatchList.title: TLS rotation procedure (78 days to expiry)
cisoPatchList.fix: Initiate renewal NOW. DNSSEC. CT monitoring. Audit Google CA + MFA. HPKP. Registrar MFA.
cisoPatchList.validation: DNSSEC verified; CT monitoring; HPKP test

cisoPatchList.title: cPanel/WHM brute-force protection
cisoPatchList.fix: Rate-limit WHM 5 fails → 30 min lockout. Non-standard port. MFA TOTP/FIDO2. Key-based auth only. Audit default accounts.
cisoPatchList.validation: Rate limit test fires; MFA enforced; WHM not externally reachable

cisoPatchList.title: ASP.NET cookie + session security — SameSite=Strict + CSRF
cisoPatchList.fix: SameSite=Strict (not Lax). Server-side CSRF token validation. [ValidateAntiForgeryToken]. CSP form-action 'self'.
cisoPatchList.validation: SameSite=Strict header; CSRF token enforced; PoC blocked

cisoPatchList.title: API endpoint security on hpclapi
cisoPatchList.fix: Rate-limit 100 req/min/IP. API key auth. HMAC-SHA256 signing. Full request log. IP allowlist if internal-only.
cisoPatchList.validation: API key required; rate limit fires; signing enforced

cisoPatchList.title: Admin endpoint security on hpcladmin — internal IPs only
cisoPatchList.fix: Internal IPs only (VPN + office). Cloudflare WAF IP control. MFA TOTP/FIDO2. Admin audit log. Re-auth on sensitive ops.
cisoPatchList.validation: External access blocked; MFA test passes

cisoPatchList.title: Shared hosting risk assessment on lserver182-ind
cisoPatchList.fix: Audit other customers on same server. Migrate to dedicated server if possible. cPanel isolation: PHP open_basedir. Shell disabled for non-admin. Malware scan.

phase2.headlineQuestion: HPCL's complete absence of HTTP hardening headers, broad SPF spoofing surface, and imminent July 2026 TLS rotation represent a critical convergence — what incident-response and forensic capabilities exist to detect and contain a sophisticated MITM attack initiated during the TLS renewal window?
